about: Cited, published-behavior evidence for comparing memory-poisoning posture across agent harnesses. Read docs/harness-comparison-methodology.md first. RULE: every non-MERIDIAN cell is either 'unpublished' (no public evidence either way — the honest default) or backed by a `source` the reader can open. 'unpublished' does NOT mean 'absent'. We never ran any competitor's code. Re-verify before launch use; OSS security posture moves weekly.

values.yes: public, checkable evidence the capability exists
values.partial: a weaker or related form exists
values.no: public evidence it explicitly lacks the capability (cited)
values.unpublished: no public evidence either way — NOT proof of absence

dimensions.label: Recall-stage screen
dimensions.question: Is recalled memory screened before it reaches the model?

dimensions.label: Provenance-aware trust
dimensions.question: Trusted vs untrusted memory origin at recall?

dimensions.label: Signed provenance
dimensions.question: Is trust cryptographic, not a spoofable label?

harnesses.name: MERIDIAN
harnesses.summary: Recall-stage two-tier defense + signed provenance + open benchmark. All cells are in-repo and reader-checkable.

recall_screen.evidence: src/verification/memory-integrity.ts screenRecall, wired in src/agent/turn.ts before the model call

signed_provenance.evidence: src/verification/provenance.ts HMAC-SHA256 per-agent signed source; config.cortex.provenanceTrust='signed'

cluster_detection.evidence: detectGradualCluster: theme + entity-linked clustering, members kept + caution injected

open_benchmark.evidence: scripts/mempoison/mempoisonbench.mts + version-controlled catalog; CI regression guard

published_number.evidence: MemPoisonBench v3: 28/28 must-quarantine 100%->0%, 0 false positives, signed mode closes 4/4 laundering; two-tier honest reporting of the encoding/semantic frontier

harnesses.name: OpenClaw
harnesses.summary: Star/usage leader. Independent research documents the exact gradual-subversion and decision-stage gaps; no public memory-poisoning defense or benchmark found.

recall_screen.evidence: No published recall-stage memory-integrity screen found; the MEMORY.md poisoning demonstrated in arXiv 2603.11619 persisted across sessions, consistent with no such screen, but no maintainer statement asserts its absence.

provenance_trust.evidence: No public provenance-trust mechanism for recalled memory documented.

cluster_detection.evidence: Independent research (Tsinghua + Ant Group): OpenClaw 'does not implement persistent monitoring to detect when legitimate context accumulation is gradually subverted'.

judge_tier.evidence: No public model-judged memory-integrity tier documented.

open_benchmark.evidence: No open memory-poisoning benchmark for OpenClaw found.

published_number.evidence: No published poisoning-resistance number found; conventional hardening shipped in May 2026 releases addressed sandbox escapes, not memory-integrity.

harnesses.name: Hermes Agent
harnesses.summary: Usage leader (#1 OpenRouter tokens per mid-2026 snapshots). Feature set / governance largely unexamined in public security literature; no memory-poisoning defense or benchmark found.

recall_screen.evidence: No public recall-stage memory screen documented.

harnesses.name: Mastra
harnesses.summary: Sets the OPEN-BENCHMARK bar for memory ACCURACY (LongMemEval, fully reproducible harness). That is a different axis from poisoning resistance; on poisoning specifically, nothing published was found.

recall_screen.evidence: No public recall-stage poisoning screen documented (its memory work targets retrieval accuracy, not adversarial integrity).

judge_tier.evidence: Uses LLM judges for accuracy eval, not for memory-integrity screening; none found for poisoning.

open_benchmark.evidence: Publishes a fully open, reproducible LongMemEval ACCURACY harness — the credibility standard to emulate — but not a poisoning benchmark.

published_number.evidence: Published numbers are LongMemEval accuracy (94.87% gpt-5-mini / 84.23% gpt-4o), not poisoning resistance.

harnesses.name: Mem0
harnesses.summary: Standalone agent-memory layer (funded). Public material centers on retrieval/accuracy and scale; no memory-poisoning defense or benchmark found.

recall_screen.evidence: None found for adversarial recall screening.

harnesses.name: Letta (MemGPT)
harnesses.summary: Memory-centric agent framework. Public material centers on memory architecture/tiering; no memory-poisoning defense or benchmark found.

recall_screen.evidence: None found for adversarial recall screening.
